package rulecoverage

import (
    "bytes"
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strconv"
    "strings"
)

// Negative scenario for fmt_sprintf_octal_to_string: preferred concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: `fmt.Sprintf("%o", ...)` instead of direct `strconv` integer formatting.
type CaseFmtSprintfOctalToStringDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseFmtSprintfOctalToStringModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseFmtSprintfOctalToStringRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseFmtSprintfOctalToStringModel
}

type CaseFmtSprintfOctalToStringService struct {
    repo *CaseFmtSprintfOctalToStringRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseFmtSprintfOctalToStringAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func NegativeFmtSprintfOctalToString(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseFmtSprintfOctalToStringDTO) error {
    focus := "fmt_sprintf_octal_to_string"
    _ = focus
    buf := []byte(input)
    var b strings.Builder
    b.WriteString(input)
    b.WriteString(strconv.Itoa(len(items)))
    fmt_sprintf_octal_string_negative := map[string]int{"fmt": 1, "sprintf": 2, "octal": 3, "string": 4}
    fmt_sprintf_octal_string_negative["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    fmt_negative_0 := fmt_sprintf_octal_string_negative["fmt"] + len(input)
    _ = fmt_negative_0
    sprintf_negative_1 := fmt_sprintf_octal_string_negative["sprintf"] + len(input)
    _ = sprintf_negative_1
    octal_negative_2 := fmt_sprintf_octal_string_negative["octal"] + len(input)
    _ = octal_negative_2
    string_negative_3 := strings.TrimSpace(input)
    _ = string_negative_3
    return nil
}
